maxwell.headline: House Resolution 119-635 formally opposes Maxwell clemency while new Epstein evidence details emerge about missing diamonds and hard drives, March 26-31, 2026
maxwell.summary: Representative Raja Krishnamoorthi introduced House Resolution 119-635 on August 5th affirming Maxwell's conviction was warranted and formally opposing any clemency, as reported in recent coverage. New testimony revealed Jeffrey Epstein's diamonds, passports, and hard drives from his safe went missing for 5 days after the 2019 FBI raid, passing through three people before returning to investigators. Additionally, Epstein's accountant and lawyer told the House Oversight Committee that government investigators never interviewed them about their work for the sex offender. BBC released new survivor interviews discussing years of abuse, with some survivors calling for release of all Epstein files.
under_radar_flags: Missing evidence chain of custody issues may complicate prosecutions
Key financial associates never interviewed suggests investigation gaps
Congressional resolution creates formal legislative opposition to clemency

iran.headline: Iran threatens US companies including Microsoft, Google, Apple starting March 31 as Hegseth declares next few days decisive for war outcome
iran.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards issued new threats on March 31, 2026, targeting 18 US companies including Microsoft, Google, Apple, Intel, IBM, Tesla and Boeing in retaliation for ongoing US-Israel strikes. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth declared the next few days would be decisive for the war outcome, warning conflict would intensify without a deal. Iran set ablaze a fully loaded oil tanker off Dubai, continuing attacks on Gulf shipping since February 28. China and Pakistan presented a new 5-point peace initiative including immediate ceasefire and Strait of Hormuz reopening, while Trump alternated between threatening to obliterate Iran's energy infrastructure and claiming negotiations were progressing through backchannel talks.
under_radar_flags: Iran targeting specific US tech companies suggests shift to economic warfare tactics
Backchannel negotiations reportedly active despite public denials from Tehran
Gulf allies urging Trump to continue pressure, saying month of strikes insufficient
Pakistan-China mediation effort indicates CRINK coordination on diplomatic track

gulf_swf.headline: Saudi PIF unveils new five-year strategy cutting capital spending 15% while pivoting to AI partnerships, with 8 IPOs planned for 2026 as announced at FII Miami March 26-27
gulf_swf.summary: Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund officially announced its new 2026-2030 strategy at the FII PRIORITY Miami conference on March 26-27, 2026, featuring a 15% reduction in capital spending and a strategic pivot toward AI investments through its subsidiary Humain, which secured its first US customer partnership with Turing. PIF Governor Al-Rumayyan confirmed the fund is shifting from predominantly self-funded mega-projects toward a broader model inviting domestic and international partners, with 8 subsidiary IPOs planned for 2026. Meanwhile, UAE's Mubadala completed multiple major transactions including the $4.75 billion exit from CoolIT Systems to Ecolab in a KKR-led deal and secured the Southwest Andaman exploration block in Indonesia with 100% participating interest. The strategic repositioning comes as Gulf SWFs navigate wartime economic pressures while maintaining global investment commitments despite Iran conflict escalation.
under_radar_flags: PIF capital spending reduction of 15% signals resource reallocation under war stress
Shift to third-party investment partnerships reduces direct sovereign exposure
Mubadala's aggressive deal-making pace (multiple billion-dollar transactions in one week) suggests accelerated portfolio optimization
AI sector pivot by both PIF (Humain) and Mubadala (data center cooling) indicates coordinated Gulf strategy

israel.headline: Israel strikes IRGC university in Tehran and destroys 100+ Beirut buildings on day 32 of war while expanding Lebanon invasion, March 31, 2026
israel.summary: Israel's military struck the Imam Hossein University in Tehran run by the IRGC, claiming it was used for advanced weapons research, marking day 32 of Operation Epic Fury. The IDF destroyed over 100 high-rise buildings in Beirut area as command structures while expanding its Lebanon invasion beyond southern regions. Israeli Defense Minister announced plans to destroy all homes in Lebanese border villages and prevent 600,000 displaced residents from returning until northern Israel is secure, indicating a prolonged occupation similar to Gaza operations. IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir coordinated next operational steps with US Central Command, stating 'the Iranian regime's axis of terror is beginning to collapse.'
under_radar_flags: IDF-US Central Command coordination of 'next steps' suggests escalation planning
Targeting of IRGC university indicates intelligence-driven precision strikes on research facilities
Gaza-like destruction promised for Lebanon indicates long-term occupation strategy
600,000 displaced Lebanese creates regional refugee crisis

oracle_ellison.headline: Oracle stock trades $139-145 range with mixed analyst ratings following Q3 2026 earnings, while AI infrastructure expansion continues amid $553B record backlog
oracle_ellison.summary: Oracle stock moved within $139.70-$145.42 range on March 31, 2026, following strong Q3 2026 earnings that showed 22% revenue growth to $17.2 billion with cloud infrastructure surging 84%. Analyst sentiment remains mixed with Bank of America issuing a Buy rating with $200 price target (29% upside) while other targets range up to $262.91. Oracle's Remaining Performance Obligations hit a record $553 billion backlog, driven by AI infrastructure demand. The company secured new Defense Information Systems Agency Impact Level 5 authorizations for government cloud services and expanded its Nashville office operations. Stock has declined 24.6% over the past three months despite strong fundamentals.
under_radar_flags: Oracle Federal Financials joins Treasury marketplace for government modernization
DISA Impact Level 5 authorization enables advanced AI deployment for government customers
Record $553B backlog suggests strong forward revenue visibility despite stock decline
Nashville office expansion indicates continued operational scaling

epstein_files.headline: DOJ releases additional Epstein documents March 30, 2026 including new Trump-related files and prison guard details, as victims sue DOJ over privacy violations
epstein_files.summary: The DOJ released a new tranche of Epstein documents on March 30, 2026, including never-before-seen files pertaining to President Trump and detailed records of prison guard activities the night Epstein died. The release reveals that prison guard Tova Noel was supposed to check on Epstein every 30 minutes but failed to do so, and includes new victim testimony from a woman alleging rape by 'Jeff' in South Carolina in the 1980s when she was 13. Multiple Epstein victims have filed lawsuits against both DOJ and Google over the release of identifying victim information, alleging the department prioritized 'volume and speed over safety and privacy.' House Oversight Chair James Comer admitted Trump's DOJ 'botched' the file release process, while AG Pam Bondi faces ongoing Congressional subpoenas over the handling.
under_radar_flags: FBI tracked 4chan users who posted about Epstein's death, obtained IP addresses via grand jury subpoena
Bank of America agreed to $72.5M settlement in Epstein case on March 28, 2026
Documents reveal previously undisclosed DEA investigation of Epstein for drug trafficking
Apollo Global Management and Leon Black face new shareholder class action lawsuit filed March 2026

arkansas_datacenter.headline: Google's $1B Little Rock data center detailed at 1.43M sq ft across 5 buildings, while Entergy announces $5B customer savings from data center agreements March 2026
arkansas_datacenter.summary: Google's $1 billion data center project at the Port of Little Rock has been detailed with specific construction plans showing five industrial buildings totaling 1.43 million square feet, two office buildings and an electrical substation, according to documents submitted to the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers. Separately, Entergy announced $5 billion in customer savings delivered by data center agreements and issued a 'Fair Share Plus' pledge, with Google specifically agreeing to support construction of a new 600-megawatt solar and 350-megawatt battery facility that will serve all Entergy Arkansas customers. This represents concrete progress on the Arkansas datacenter capital deployment following the established Strategic Investment Rider framework under Act 373.
under_radar_flags: Google data center construction documents filed with Army Corps of Engineers suggest federal permitting coordination
Entergy's 'Fair Share Plus' pledge language indicates potential response to rate structure criticism
600MW solar + 350MW battery facility represents significant grid infrastructure expansion beyond datacenter load
